audit_items.proof_trace: The loop at L228 iterates every entry in `s_allowlistedAssets` with no upper bound; neither `applyAllowlistedAssetUpdates` (FeeAggregator.sol:L319) nor `applyFeedInfoUpdates` (PriceManager.sol:L285) enforces a maximum asset count. Each iteration may invoke an external `latestRoundData()` oracle call plus two `IERC20.balanceOf` calls, making total gas O(N × 3 external calls). Because `checkUpkeep` is an off-chain Automation simulation, the binding limit is the Automation registry's `checkGasLimit` (~6.5M gas); with enough assets this limit is exceeded and `upkeepNeeded` is never returned, permanently disabling automated upkeep.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An admin holding `ASSET_ADMIN_ROLE` legitimately adds many assets to the allowlist, each backed by a Chainlink data feed. When the Automation keeper simulates `checkUpkeep`, the accumulated external oracle and `balanceOf` calls exceed the registry's `checkGasLimit`, causing the simulation to fail and `performUpkeep` to never be triggered, halting all automated auction management.
